N-PROFILE.notes: PARTIAL. The frozen mini W8A8 vector now has a complete local reference identity including a rejecting byte tokenizer; all 17 semantic op families execute, every recorded GEMM agrees with an independently ordered column-stream accumulator in forward and reverse-inner schedules, invalid tokenizer bytes reject, and identical work is co-batch invariant. This remains mini shape. The registered Qwen2.5-0.5B tokenizer/model, preregistered accuracy tolerance, two independently authored CPU implementations, certified AMD and NVIDIA kernels, and >=10^9 clean cross-vendor instances remain external.

E-NEL-01.notes: PARTIAL. The deterministic local conformance precursor covers tokenizer rejection, every W8A8 semantic op family, two reduction schedules, co-batch invariance, overflow boundaries, ties, cache-side operands, and the frozen forward vector with zero local mismatches. No claim is made for independent CPU authorship, AMD/NVIDIA execution, pinned live GPU firmware/toolchains, or the >=10^9-instance campaign; one unexplained future mismatch still kills the immutable profile.

E-NEL-01a.notes: PARTIAL. The runner validates the exact existing measured artifacts at C:/tmp/nel-real-inference/runs: 24/24 layer digests and final logits equal, 3,502,080 SiLU activations, frozen LUT max/mean error and 41 clips, NEL-L0 1,166 clips, and token-7 pair drift below recorded FP cross-implementation noise with 16/16 Vulkan token agreement. Artifact hashes are generated by the command. The runner does not certify independent authorship and presents no GPU integer or NVIDIA evidence, so E-NEL-01 is unchanged.

E-NEL-02.notes: PARTIAL. A fail-closed integer-ppm accuracy contract binds source, quantization, calibration, task and safety sets, power, tolerance, registration time, and hidden-suite opening time; it rejects post-open or missing registration, task suppression, duplicate tasks, and underpowered results, and reports every per-task loss before deciding the frozen tolerance. The harness uses an explicitly labeled fixture only. Real registered 0.5B source/W8A8 evaluation on the unopened hidden suite remains external.

E-NEL-03.notes: PARTIAL. The local label/latency state machine rejects any non-SOFT surface, computes deterministic committed-token p50/p95/p99 from forward+quorum only, retains anchoring/on-chain/proof fields separately, counts disagreements, and pairs drops with refunds. A 100-sample fixture exercises three labeled clusters and the laundering falsifier, but is not a public latency experiment. Three independent live control clusters and preregistered load/fault observations remain external.

E-NEL-04.notes: PARTIAL. A real local T=32 committed execution corrupts one layer-1 DOWN accumulator at position 31, the existing token-layer-op referee isolates it and faults the executor, an honest unrelated job continues, and a frivolous challenger loses. Deadline, malformed, late, availability-pause, and forced-move falsifiers remain in the crate suite; the frozen D=25 and declared 19-round/40-transaction public path are machine-bound. Public-testnet collusion/censorship timing and two sustainable independent challengers remain external.

E-NEL-05.notes: PARTIAL. Every local model-weight byte and every layer-activation byte is namespace-bound, systematic-sharded with XOR parity across distinct operator identities, reconstructed after one loss, and unavailable after correlated loss so assurance cannot start. Two canonical executions are probed at 10^4 deterministic randomized KV positions with zero root mismatch; poison and cross-job cache reuse reject. The 4-16 MiB production shard schema is enforced. Real 0.5B shards, operator-diverse public custody, 99.9% retrieval for 30 days, and the production replay SLA remain external.

E-NEL-06.notes: PARTIAL. The specialized-proof benchmark interface requires public-hardware provenance, >=0.5B parameters, 16-32 tokens, named hardware, pinned circuit/compiler commits, witness memory, wall/energy/cost, proof bytes, aggregation/verifier timing, failures, challenge-window completion, and two differently named verifiers with identical results. Fixtures and extrapolations reject before economics; >10x cost, late proof, or verifier >=1s reject. No specialized proof was run and SpecializedChunkV1 stays disabled.

E-NEL-07.notes: PARTIAL. The commit-before-beacon state machine fixes a unique threshold output after execution commitments close, rejects post-reveal commitments and alternate outputs, consumes each draw tuple once, and maps withholding only to bounded refund. The deterministic study executes 10^6 adversarial schedules with zero accepted post-reveal commitments, alternate beacons, or replayed draws; greedy state cursors remain zero and the integer sampler is exercised. Independent second-client authorship, a live threshold-beacon fault drill, and the 30-day <1% stall observation remain external.

notes: NelLabClaims adds claim-specific executable local precursors, raw generated evidence, falsifiers, and machine-readable external prerequisites for the assigned NEL cluster. All nine proposals remain PARTIAL because their exact frozen thresholds include real-model, independent-vendor/client, public-network/duration, specialized-hardware, or independent-review requirements that this worktree cannot satisfy.
